name: boneio
documentation:
Example usage:
boneio run -dd -c config.yaml

# Installation instructions

```
sudo apt-get install libopenjp2-7-dev libatlas-base-dev python3-venv libjpeg-dev zlib1g-dev
mkdir ~/boneio
python3 -m venv ~/boneio/venv
source ~/boneio/venv/bin/activate
pip3 install --upgrade boneio
cp ~/venv/lib/python3.7/site-packages/boneio/example_config/*.yaml ~/boneio/
```

Edit config.yaml

# Start app

```
source ~/boneio/venv/bin/activate
boneio run -c ~/boneio/config.yaml -dd
```

methods.name: boneio.helper.yaml_util.CustomValidator._lookup_field
documentation:
Implement relative paths with dot (.) notation, following Python
guidelines: https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0328/#guido-s-decision
- A single leading dot indicates a relative import
starting with the current package.
- Two or more leading dots give a relative import to the parent(s)
of the current package, one level per dot after the first
Return: Tuple(dependency_name: str, dependency_value: Any)

modules.name: boneio.helper.queue
documentation:
Special unique implementation of asyncio.Queue for boneIO.
If MQTT is down then regular queue can append multiple ON/OFF for same topic.
After re-connection it would send all messages. It's not necessary, last payload of same topic is enough.

methods.name: boneio.helper.queue.UniqueQueue._put
documentation:
If item does not exists add it.
If exists remove old one and put new one in the beginning of the queue.
